title: 3.2 Custom Tenant Provisioning & Prompt Engineering
lecture:
1. Conceptual Breakdown (The "What" and "Why")

A single AI Funnel Machine instance can serve a plumbing company in Las Vegas, a roofing contractor in Texas, and a property manager in Florida simultaneously. This is called Multi-Tenant Software. To make this work, the system must load different behaviors, voices, and instructions dynamically depending on who is calling.

Instead of hardcoding separate prompts or deploying separate servers for each client (which is expensive and hard to manage), we use **SQLite Dynamic Provisioning** and **System Prompt Engineering**:

  • Tenant Partitioning: Every client Settings record has a unique client_id. When a phone call hits Twilio or a chat widget opens, the request passes this ID.
  • Prompt Templates: We design system prompt templates that incorporate client-specific variables (company name, service offerings, greeting text, call instructions).
  • Model Configuration Overrides: We store custom system instructions and voice parameters in the database. When the agent session initializes, the system loads these overrides, changing the AI's behavior in real-time.

2. Code-Level & File Walkthrough

Custom provisioning and dynamic prompt loading are managed in:

  • [index_new.ts](file:///Users/ashleybutler/ai-funnel-machine/src/index_new.ts): Contains the client config resolvers and fallback system prompts.
  • [leadGuard.ts](file:///Users/ashleybutler/ai-funnel-machine/src/outbound/leadGuard.ts): Provisions the database schema for the client_settings table.

Let's look at the niche prompt resolver in index_new.ts:

export function getVoiceAgentInstructions(clientId: string): string {
  const settings = leadGuard.getClientSettings(clientId);
  if (settings && settings.voiceInstructions) {
    return settings.voiceInstructions;
  }
  const config = CLIENT_CONFIGS[clientId] || CLIENT_CONFIGS.default_client;
  
  if (clientId === 'realestate_nexus') {
    return `You are an elite, professional, and friendly real estate booking assistant for ${config.name}...`;
  }
  // default niche prompts...
}

Line-by-Line Breakdown:

  • Lines 1-4: The code queries the SQLite database for the client settings. If the database has a custom voiceInstructions override, it returns it immediately. This allows us to update agent prompts in real-time without redeploying code.
  • Lines 5-7: If no database record is found, it falls back to the hardcoded config map (CLIENT_CONFIGS).
  • Lines 8-11: It evaluates the client's industry sector and returns a pre-engineered prompt template, injecting the client's company name (e.g. `${config.name}`). This prompt controls the agent's tone, rules, and conversation goals.
exercise:
3. Step-by-Step Guided Exercise

Provision a new client in the database and verify that prompt overrides resolve correctly.

  • Step A: Review the default client configs in src/index_new.ts (lines 36-67) to see the hardcoded structures.
  • Step B: Open your terminal in /Users/ashleybutler/ai-funnel-machine.
  • Step C: Execute the verification script:
    • npx tsx verify_voice_settings.ts
  • Step D: Inspect the logs. Check that Case 4 (Default Niche Prompt Fallback) executes successfully, demonstrating that the system resolves the correct instructions even if no database override row exists.

quizzes.question: What is the primary benefit of multi-tenant database-driven prompt engineering?
options: It speeds up the Vercel deployment time.
It allows you to serve multiple clients with distinct business rules using a single server deployment by loading configurations dynamically from the database.
It eliminates the need for any AI models.
It encrypts the customer's phone number.
quizzes.explanation: Dynamic provisioning pulls configs from SQLite at runtime. This allows you to scale to hundreds of clients on a single server without having to rewrite or redeploy code for each tenant.

quizzes.question: Which database table stores the custom system instructions and voice settings for each client?
options: lead_registry
client_settings
call_logs
knowledge_base
quizzes.explanation: The client_settings table is structured to store all tenant-specific details, including phone numbers, greetings, calendars, and voice prompts.

quizzes.question: What happens if getVoiceAgentInstructions finds a custom string in the 'voice_instructions' database field?
options: It throws an error and falls back to default settings.
It overrides all system defaults and returns the custom instruction string directly to the AI connection.
It deletes the row from SQLite.
It shuts down the server.
quizzes.explanation: If a custom database override exists, the system prioritizes it and returns it immediately, bypassing default templates.

quizzes.question: In the getVoiceAgentInstructions function, what is the default fallback if no database record or client config matches the ID?
options: It uses realestate_nexus settings.
It falls back to the default_client configuration (Syncro Scale).
It returns an empty string.
It crashes the server.
quizzes.explanation: To prevent runtime crashes, the resolver falls back to the default_client configuration (Syncro Scale) if no matching client configs are found.

quizzes.question: How does the system ensure that the AI model does not step out of bounds (e.g. quote prices or discuss competition)?
options: By deleting the OpenAI API key.
By enforcing strict system prompts with explicit rules ('do not quote prices, direct users to estimators') and validating outputs.
By limiting the length of the user's responses.
By running on a local offline container.
quizzes.explanation: Explicit constraints within system prompts ('guardrails') instruct the model on what it is not allowed to say, mitigating the risk of hallucinations.
